_note: Round 5b — synthetic findings to verify multi-page StrikeOut + /IRT linking. Includes (a) a delete finding whose source_text spans the page 2→3 boundary in msa_reasoning_test.pdf, and (b) two single-page regression cases from Round 5a (one single-line, one multi-line) to confirm no regression.

findings._note: Source text 'Fees do not include any taxes, levies, duties, or similar governmental assessments' starts on page 2 (last line) and continues on page 3 (first line). Should produce TWO StrikeOut annotations linked by /IRT.
findings.source_text: Fees do not include any taxes, levies, duties, or similar governmental assessments
findings.external_comment: Tax-exclusion language is overly broad. Buyer's tax allocation playbook requires specific carve-outs for value-added taxes, withholding taxes, and jurisdictional taxes — recommend striking and replacing with negotiated tax-allocation provision.

findings._note: Round 5a baseline single-line case — should still produce ONE StrikeOut annotation with ONE QuadPoints quad (no IRT, no group).
findings.external_comment: Vendor-favorable absolute-discretion language. Strike to require a reasonableness standard.

findings._note: Round 5a fix #4 multi-line single-page case — should produce ONE StrikeOut annotation with N QuadPoints quads (where N matches visual line count), still no IRT.
findings.source_text: Lattice's sole obligations and Customer's sole and exclusive remedies for failures to meet such service-level commitments are the service credits described in Exhibit A.
findings.external_comment: Service credits as the SOLE remedy is too restrictive — buyer should retain access to broader contractual remedies (termination, damages) for material service-level failures.
